functions.name: deepview.rt.common.strerror

functions.name: deepview.rt.common.nativetype
documentation:
Converts the Numpy type definition to the deepview-rt NNTensorType enum.
This function will convert known mappings between Numpy and DeepViewRT but
even if a mapping is known usage may still fail with
TensorTypeUnsupportedError if the actual operation does not support this
type.

Note:
    Currently DeepViewRT ONLY supports numpy.float32.

Returns:
    int: Native (C ABI) NNTensorType matching the Numpy datatype.

Raises:
    TensorTypeUnsupportedError: If the Numpy datatype is not supported.

modules.name: deepview.rt
documentation:
DeepViewRT for Python provides Python bindings for the target run-time of the
DeepView Machine Learning Toolkit implementing neural network primitives and
model inference support.  The Python bindings are implemented using the CFFI
module to interact directly with the DeepViewRT C ABI.  To use this module,
libdeepview-rt.so needs to be available in the library search path along with
any engines that are to be used (ex: deepview-rt-opencl.so).  On Windows the
library will be called deepview-rt.dll and on MacOSX it will be called
libdeepview-rt.dylib, otherwise the usage remains the same.
Copyright 2018 by Au-Zone Technologies.  All Rights Reserved.
Unauthorized copying of this file, via any medium is strictly prohibited
Proprietary and confidential.
This source code is provided solely for runtime interpretation by Python.
Modifying or copying any source code is explicitly forbidden.

classes.name: deepview.rt.errors.Error
documentation:
Error provides the base Exception class for DeepViewRT.  All common errors
subclass this class to provide specific NNError to Python Exception maps.
The underlying NNError code can be read from Error.code() and the human
readable string can be read using Error.error_string().

classes.name: deepview.rt.errors.ShapePropagationError

methods.name: deepview.rt.errors.ShapePropagationError.__init__
documentation:
Constructor
\:param nxgraph: Graph containing model up to the error point
\:param node_name: Name of node where shape propagation error occured
\:param get_subgraph: Boolean to get subgraph of unknown nodes
\:param draw_subgraph: Boolean to plot graph of unknown nodes

methods.name: deepview.rt.errors.ShapePropagationError.draw_subgraph
documentation:
Draws subgraph of unknown nodes using matplotlib
\:param nxgraph: Subgraph of unknown nodes
\:return:

methods.name: deepview.rt.errors.ShapePropagationError.get_subgraph
documentation:
Generates subgraph of unknown nodes recursively
\:param nxgraph: Graph of entire model up to failure point
\:param node_name: Name of failure node
\:return:

methods.name: deepview.rt.errors.ShapePropagationError.find_known_node
documentation:
Generate dict recursively from failure node to last known node
\:param nxgraph: Graph of entire model up to failure point
\:param node_name: Name of failure node
\:return:

Traces up to last known node for each unknown node and stores the nodes
in a dict of dicts. Key for outer dict is the failed shape prop node
name and the value is a dict containing that node's inputs (probably
unknown nodes) Key for inner dict is the unknown node's name and the
value is that node's info. e.g. if shape prop fails on node y,
then node_traceback[y] will be a dict containing y's inputs, say x_1,
x_2, ... if x_i is an unknown node, then node_traceback[x_i] will be
x_i's inputs and so on until there is a known layer

modules.name: deepview.rt.ops
documentation:
The pattern to all functions is to return the output tensor and optionally
accept the output tensor as parameter "out", when this parameter is not
provided it will be created with the appropriate setup (shape, datatype, and
engine).  Since the function will return the out parameter regardless it can be
used for lazy initialization as in the following example, here the c tensor is
created by the add() function and reused by the subtract() function.

Example:

```python c = None a = deepview.rt.Tensor(numpy.random.rand(1)) b = deepview.rt.Tensor(numpy.random.rand(1)) c = deepview.rt.ops.add(a, b, c) c = deepview.rt.ops.subtract(a, b, c)

```

In cases where we wish to experiment with custom graphs without using RTM
models they could be represented, for example, using the networkx module by
having vertices representing the ops and edges representing the tensors.  In
this case the out tensors would be the edge leaving a vertex which would then
be reused by the vertex on the other side of the edge as an input.
Copyright 2018 by Au-Zone Technologies.  All Rights Reserved.
Unauthorized copying of this file, via any medium is strictly prohibited
Proprietary and confidential.
This source code is provided solely for runtime interpretation by Python.
Modifying or copying any source code is explicitly forbidden.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.ssd.get_bounding_box
documentation:
Retrieves the detections from an SSD image detection model, parses the results and returns it as a list

Parameters:
----------
context: Context
    The DeepViewRT Context to get the bounding box detections for. It should already have
    an SSD detection model loaded, with image data loaded into the input tensor and inference with
    `Context.run()`

anchor_type: Optional[int]
    The expected SSD anchor type of the model loaded in the DeepViewRT Context. If `None`, the anchor
    type will be automatically calculated

iou: float
    The IoU threshold for NMS in the range 0..1

max_boxes: int
    The maximum number of bounding boxes

threshold: float
    Threshold for valid boxes in the range of 0..1

Returns:
----------
results: List[Detection]
    The results are returned as list of Detections.

    The Detection tuple is (label, bbox_coordinates, score).

    The bounding box coordinates are normalized between 0 to 1. The coordinates are given as 
    `(corner0.x, corner0.y, corner2.x, corner2.y)`, where `corner0` is the top left, and `corner2`
    is the bottom right.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.ssd.decode_nms_standard_bbx
documentation:
Decodes the results for a standard anchor type SSD model.

Returns
----------
    boxes tensor: Tensor
        holds the information about the bounding boxes detected,
    classes_found: Tensor
        contains the number of detections of each class

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.ssd.decode_nms_variance_bbx
documentation:
Decodes the results for a variance anchor type SSD model.

Returns
----------
    boxes tensor: Tensor
        holds the information about the bounding boxes detected,
    classes_found: Tensor
        contains the number of detections of each class

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.ssd.calculate_anchor_type
documentation:
Returns
----------
    anchor_type: int
        the calculated anchor type for the Context

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.ssd.set_anchor_type
documentation: Sets the aux object "anchor_type" to the type given

variables.name: deepview.rt.ops.ssd.Bounding_Box_Coordinate
documentation:
The normalized coordinates of the bounding box of a detection. The coordinates are given as 
`(corner0.x, corner0.y, corner2.x, corner2.y)`, where corner0 is the top left, and corner2 is the bottom right.

variables.name: deepview.rt.ops.ssd.Detection
documentation:
A single detection of an ssd detection model. Contains the label (`str`), bounding box coordinates 
(`tuple(float, float, float, float)`), and score (`float`)

This source code is provided solely for runtime interpretation by Python.

classes.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.__init__
documentation:
Constructs a DeepViewRT tensor.  If copy_from is set to a valid Numpy
array it will be mapped into the Tensor and a reference will be held
to ensure validity across the lifespan of the tensor, if shape is also
provided the tensor will be reshaped accordingly.

Note that though copy_from will be mapped to the Tensor there's no
guarantee that the memory will be used beyond initialization of a
Engine backed tensor as this is defined by the specific engine
implementation.  As an alternative consider a role reversal where the
tensor owns the data and a numpy array is retrieved through tensor's
map/unmap methods.

If copy_from is None then shape can be used to allocate a zero
initialized tensor. If neither copy_from nor shape is provided then an
empty tensor is created.

The engine parameter can be used to create a Tensor using the
specified engine plugin.

Args:
    copy_from (Optional[numpy.ndarray]):
        Create a tensor mapped from the specified numpy array.
    shape (Optional[List[int]]):
        Shape (or reshape, if copy_from is provided) of the tensor.
    engine (Optional[Engine]):
        Create the tensor using this engine.
    dtype (numpy.dtype):
        Sets the underlying datatype, only numpy.float32 is currently
        supported.

Raises:
    TensorTypeUnsupportedError:
        If dtype is an unknown or unsupported type.
    Error:
        If an internal error occurs.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.engine
documentation:
The tensor's engine.

Returns:
    Engine: Engine or None if no engine is associated to tensor.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.engine_name
documentation:
The tensor's engine's name.

Returns:
    str: Engine name or None if no engine is associated to tensor.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.engine_version
documentation:
The tensor's engine's version number.

Returns:
    str:
        Engine version string or None if no engine is associated to
        tensor.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.volume
documentation:
Volume of the tensor's data which is the inner product of it's shape.

Returns:
    int: The volume of the tensor.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.element_size
documentation:
Size in bytes of an individual tensor data element.

Returns:
    int: Element size of the tensor.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.size
documentation:
Size in bytes of the tensor's data, this is equivalent to
volume * element_size.

Returns:
    int: The tensor's data size in bytes.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.dims
documentation:
Number of dimensions for this tensor.

Returns:
    int: the number of dimensions for the tensor.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.shape
documentation:
Shape of the tensor's data.

Returns:
    Tuple[int, ...]: The shape of the tensor.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.reshape
documentation:
Reshapes the tensor representation, new shape must have the same
volume as previous.

Raises:
    Error: If reshape operation fails.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.sync
documentation:
Synchronize the tensor and preceeding operations.

Raises:
    Error: If synchronization fails.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.map
documentation:
Maps the tensor and returns a Numpy array map over the underlying
buffer. The tensor must be unmapped once no longer needed, note that
extreme care must be taken if calling unmap while the Numpy array
continues to exist with a now invalid pointer.  Another option is to
use the array() method which will return a copy of the tensor's data
which is safer though slower.

Args:
    writeable (bool):
        If true the tensor is mapped as writeable otherwise read-only

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: A Numpy array mapped to the tensor's data.

Raises:
    TensorNoDataError:
        If the map failed, likely because the tensor has no data.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.unmap
documentation:
Unmaps the tensor.

Raises:
    Error: on internal error.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.array
documentation:
Returns a copy of the tensor's data as a new numpy array.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: Numpy array of the tensor's data

Raises:
    TensorNoDataError: if the tensor has no data.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.copy_from
documentation: Copies the Numpy array into the Tensor

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.fill
documentation:
Fills the tensor with the provided value which will be casted
to double then eventually to the tensor's data type.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.load_image
documentation:
Loads an image into the tensor using PIL and numpy

Parameters
----------
image_fp: str | bytes | Path | SupportsRead[Bytes]
    A filename or path object or file object of the image to load into the tensor

img_proc: int
    The image processing flags to use

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.scales
documentation:
Returns the quantization scales for the tensor or None if the tensor is
not quantized.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.zeros
documentation:
Returns the quantization scales for the tensor or None if the tensor is
not quantized.

methods.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.dequantize
documentation:
This function performs tensor dequantization

Parameters
----------
x: Tensor | defaults to None
   Output Tensor. If x is not None,
   the dequantization results will be copy into x,
   otherwise it will be returned

Returns
-------
Tensor
   This function will return a tensor
   if x is not None, otherwise, dequantization results
   will be returned

inner_classes.name: deepview.rt.tensor.Tensor.IMG_PROC
documentation: Flags for how image preprocessing should be done

Tensor objects are the primary means of holding data within DeepViewRT.
All operations use data through tensor objects.

Shapes are currently limited to a maximum of 4 dimensions, attempting to
use more dimensions will raise an exception.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.conv.conv
documentation:
conv

Args:
    inp (Tensor): The input tensor.
    out (Optional[Tensor]):
        Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:
    Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:
    Error: if an error happened.

plugins such as the OpenCL engine (deepview-rt-opencl.so).  It can then be
used to initialize Tensors with this engine.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.activation.sigmoid
documentation:
$$
out = \frac { 1 } { 1 + e^-x }
$$

Args:

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.Offset
documentation:
Offset provides access into the Table's vtable.

Deprecated fields are ignored by checking the vtable's length.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.Indirect
documentation: Indirect retrieves the relative offset stored at `offset`.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.String
documentation: String gets a string from data stored inside the flatbuffer.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.VectorLen
documentation:
VectorLen retrieves the length of the vector whose offset is stored
at "off" in this object.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.Vector
documentation:
Vector retrieves the start of data of the vector whose offset is
stored at "off" in this object.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.Union
documentation:
Union initializes any Table-derived type to point to the union at
the given offset.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.Get
documentation:
Get retrieves a value of the type specified by `flags`  at the
given offset.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.GetVectorAsNumpy
documentation:
GetVectorAsNumpy returns the vector that starts at `Vector(off)`
as a numpy array with the type specified by `flags`. The array is
a `view` into Bytes, so modifying the returned array will
modify Bytes in place.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.table.Table.GetVOffsetTSlot
documentation:
GetVOffsetTSlot retrieves the VOffsetT that the given vtable location
points to. If the vtable value is zero, the default value `d`
will be returned.

Table wraps a byte slice and provides read access to its data.

The variable `Pos` indicates the root of the FlatBuffers object therein.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.add
documentation:
Add two tensors together, storing the results into the out tensor.  If
out is None it will be created with the expected shape of the output.
In either case the resulting out tensor is returned.

Args:
    a (Tensor): The left-hand tensor.
    b (Tensor): The right-hand tensor.
    out (Optional[Tensor]):
        Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:
    Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:
    Error: if an error happened.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.subtract
documentation:
Subtract the b tensor from the a tensor, storing the results into the out
tensor. If out is None it will be created with the expected shape of the
output. In either case the resulting out tensor is returned.

Args:
    a (Tensor): The left-hand tensor.
    b (Tensor): The right-hand tensor.
    out (Optional[Tensor]):
        Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:
    Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:
    Error: if an error happened.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.multiply
documentation:
Multiply the a and b tensors, storing the results into the out tensor.
If out is None it will be created with the expected shape of the output.
In either case the resulting out tensor is returned.

Args:
    a (Tensor): The left-hand tensor.
    b (Tensor): The right-hand tensor.
    out (Optional[Tensor]):
        Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:
    Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:
    Error: if an error happened.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.divide
documentation:
$$
c = \frac { a } { b }
$$

Divide the a tensor by the b tensor, storing the results into the out
tensor. If out is None it will be created with the expected shape of the
output. In either case the resulting out tensor is returned.

Args:

: a (Tensor): The left-hand tensor.
  b (Tensor): The right-hand tensor.
  out (Optional\[Tensor\]):

  > Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:

: Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:

: Error: if an error happened.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.abs
documentation:
$$
out = |x|
$$

Calculate the absolute value of each element of the tensor.

Args:

: x (Tensor): The input tensor.
  out (Optional\[Tensor\]):

  > Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:

: Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:

: Error: if an error happened.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.matmul
documentation:
$$
out = matmul(a,b)
$$

Calculate the matrix multiplication of A and B.

Args:

: a (Tensor): The A tensor.
  b (Tensor): The B tensor.
  out (Optional\[Tensor\]):

  > Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.dense
documentation:
$$
out = dense(x, w, b)
$$

Calculate the matrix multiplication of x and w plus bias b.

Args:

: x (Tensor): The A tensor.
  w (Tensor): The B tensor, transposed.
  b (Optional\[Tensor\]): The optional bias.
  activation (Optional\[Activation\]): The optional activation function.
  out (Optional\[Tensor\]):

  > Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:

: Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:

: Error: if an error happened.

functions.name: deepview.rt.ops.math.linear
documentation:
$$
out = linear(x, w, b)
$$

Calculate the matrix multiplication of x and wT (transposed) plus bias b.

Args:

: x (Tensor): The A tensor.
  w (Tensor): The B tensor, transposed.
  b (Optional\[Tensor\]): The optional bias.
  activation (Optional\[Activation\]): The optional activation function.
  out (Optional\[Tensor\]):

  > Optional result tensor, if not provided it will be created.

Returns:

: Tensor: The out tensor holding the results of the operation.

Raises:

: Error: if an error happened.

functions.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.vtableEqual
documentation: vtableEqual compares an unwritten vtable to a written vtable.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.OffsetArithmeticError
documentation:
Error caused by an Offset arithmetic error. Probably caused by bad
writing of fields. This is considered an unreachable situation in
normal circumstances.
# @file
# @addtogroup flatbuffers_python_api
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.IsNotNestedError
documentation:
Error caused by using a Builder to write Object data when not inside
an Object.

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.IsNestedError
documentation:
Error caused by using a Builder to begin an Object when an Object is
already being built.

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.StructIsNotInlineError
documentation:
Error caused by using a Builder to write a Struct at a location that
is not the current Offset.

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.BuilderSizeError
documentation:
Error caused by causing a Builder to exceed the hardcoded limit of 2
gigabytes.

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.BuilderNotFinishedError
documentation: Error caused by not calling `Finish` before calling `Output`.

classes.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a Builder of size `initial_size`.

The internal buffer is grown as needed.
# @endcond

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Output
documentation:
Return the portion of the buffer that has been used for writing data.

This is the typical way to access the FlatBuffer data inside the
builder. If you try to access `Builder.Bytes` directly, you would need
to manually index it with `Head()`, since the buffer is constructed
backwards.

It raises BuilderNotFinishedError if the buffer has not been finished
with `Finish`.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.StartObject
documentation: StartObject initializes bookkeeping for writing a new object.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.WriteVtable
documentation:
WriteVtable serializes the vtable for the current object, if needed.

Before writing out the vtable, this checks pre-existing vtables for
equality to this one. If an equal vtable is found, point the object to
the existing vtable and return.

Because vtable values are sensitive to alignment of object data, not
all logically-equal vtables will be deduplicated.

A vtable has the following format:
  <VOffsetT: size of the vtable in bytes, including this value>
  <VOffsetT: size of the object in bytes, including the vtable offset>
  <VOffsetT: offset for a field> * N, where N is the number of fields
             in the schema for this type. Includes deprecated fields.
Thus, a vtable is made of 2 + N elements, each VOffsetT bytes wide.

An object has the following format:
  <SOffsetT: offset to this object's vtable (may be negative)>
  <byte: data>+

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.EndObject
documentation: EndObject writes data necessary to finish object construction.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.growByteBuffer
documentation:
Doubles the size of the byteslice, and copies the old data towards
the end of the new buffer (since we build the buffer backwards).

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Head
documentation:
Get the start of useful data in the underlying byte buffer.

Note: unlike other functions, this value is interpreted as from the
left.
# @endcond

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Offset
documentation: Offset relative to the end of the buffer.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Pad
documentation: Pad places zeros at the current offset.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Prep
documentation:
Prep prepares to write an element of `size` after `additional_bytes`
have been written, e.g. if you write a string, you need to align
such the int length field is aligned to SizeInt32, and the string
data follows it directly.
If all you need to do is align, `additionalBytes` will be 0.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependSOffsetTRelative
documentation:
PrependSOffsetTRelative prepends an SOffsetT, relative to where it
will be written.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUOffsetTRelative
documentation:
Prepends an unsigned offset into vector data, relative to where it
will be written.
# @endcond

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.StartVector
documentation:
StartVector initializes bookkeeping for writing a new vector.

A vector has the following format:
  - <UOffsetT: number of elements in this vector>
  - <T: data>+, where T is the type of elements of this vector.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.EndVector
documentation: EndVector writes data necessary to finish vector construction.
# @endcond

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.CreateString
documentation: CreateString writes a null-terminated byte string as a vector.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.CreateByteVector
documentation: CreateString writes a byte vector.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.assertNested
documentation: Check that we are in the process of building an object.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.assertNotNested
documentation:
Check that no other objects are being built while making this
object. If not, raise an exception.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.assertStructIsInline
documentation:
Structs are always stored inline, so need to be created right
where they are used. You'll get this error if you created it
elsewhere.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Slot
documentation:
Slot sets the vtable key `voffset` to the current location in the
buffer.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.__Finish
documentation: Finish finalizes a buffer, pointing to the given `rootTable`.
# @endcond

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Finish
documentation: Finish finalizes a buffer, pointing to the given `rootTable`.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.FinishSizePrefixed
documentation:
Finish finalizes a buffer, pointing to the given `rootTable`,
with the size prefixed.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Prepend
documentation: # @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUOffsetTRelativeSlot
documentation:
PrependUOffsetTRelativeSlot prepends an UOffsetT onto the object at
vtable slot `o`. If value `x` equals default `d`, then the slot will
be set to zero and no other data will be written.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependStructSlot
documentation:
PrependStructSlot prepends a struct onto the object at vtable slot `o`.
Structs are stored inline, so nothing additional is being added.
In generated code, `d` is always 0.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependBool
documentation:
Prepend a `bool` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.
# @endcond

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependByte
documentation:
Prepend a `byte` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint8
documentation:
Prepend an `uint8` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint16
documentation:
Prepend an `uint16` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint32
documentation:
Prepend an `uint32` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint64
documentation:
Prepend an `uint64` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt8
documentation:
Prepend an `int8` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt16
documentation:
Prepend an `int16` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt32
documentation:
Prepend an `int32` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt64
documentation:
Prepend an `int64` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependFloat32
documentation:
Prepend a `float32` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependFloat64
documentation:
Prepend a `float64` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependVOffsetT
documentation: # @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Place
documentation:
Place prepends a value specified by `flags` to the Builder,
without checking for available space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PlaceVOffsetT
documentation:
PlaceVOffsetT prepends a VOffsetT to the Builder, without checking
for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PlaceSOffsetT
documentation:
PlaceSOffsetT prepends a SOffsetT to the Builder, without checking
for space.

methods.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PlaceUOffsetT
documentation:
PlaceUOffsetT prepends a UOffsetT to the Builder, without checking
for space.

class_variables.name: deepview.flatbuffers.builder.Builder.__slots__
documentation:
Maximum buffer size constant, in bytes.

Builder will never allow it's buffer grow over this size.
Currently equals 2Gb.

A Builder is used to construct one or more FlatBuffers.

Typically, Builder objects will be used from code generated by the `flatc`
compiler.

A Builder constructs byte buffers in a last-first manner for simplicity and
performance during reading.

Internally, a Builder is a state machine for creating FlatBuffer objects.

It holds the following internal state:
    - Bytes: an array of bytes.
    - current_vtable: a list of integers.
    - vtables: a list of vtable entries (i.e. a list of list of integers).

Attributes:
  Bytes: The internal `bytearray` for the Builder.
  finished: A boolean determining if the Builder has been finalized.
# @endcond
